result.stdout:
Description:
  Easy Digital Signing Tool

Usage:
  esign [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <bundle> <files>  Create new bundle or update an existing one
  info <bundle>         Show bundle information
  sign <bundle>         Sign bundle with certificate
  verify <bundle>       Verify bundle
  config <key> <value>  Get or set configuration values
  self-sign             Generate self-signed root CA
  trust                 Manage trusted root CAs and intermediate CAs

commands.payload:
Description:
  Easy Digital Signing Tool

Usage:
  esign [command] [options]

Options:
  --version       Show version information
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <bundle> <files>  Create new bundle or update an existing one
  info <bundle>         Show bundle information
  sign <bundle>         Sign bundle with certificate
  verify <bundle>       Verify bundle
  config <key> <value>  Get or set configuration values
  self-sign             Generate self-signed root CA
  trust                 Manage trusted root CAs and intermediate CAs

result.stdout:
Description:
  Create new bundle or update an existing one

Usage:
  esign add [<bundle> [<files>...]] [options]

Arguments:
  <bundle>  Bundle path or directory containing the bundle
            if the bundle name is not specified, a default name will be used
  <files>   Files to add to the bundle, Must be inside the bundle root path
            if not specified, all files in the bundle root path will be added

Options:
  -r, --replace    Replace existing entries
  -R, --recursive  Add all files within the bundle root path recursively
  -c, --continue   Continue adding files if an error occurs
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Create new bundle or update an existing one

Usage:
  esign add [<bundle> [<files>...]] [options]

Arguments:
  <bundle>  Bundle path or directory containing the bundle
            if the bundle name is not specified, a default name will be used
  <files>   Files to add to the bundle, Must be inside the bundle root path
            if not specified, all files in the bundle root path will be added

Options:
  -r, --replace    Replace existing entries
  -R, --recursive  Add all files within the bundle root path recursively
  -c, --continue   Continue adding files if an error occurs
  -?, -h, --help   Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Get or set configuration values

Usage:
  esign config [<key> [<value>]] [options]

Arguments:
  <key>    Key to set or get
           if not specified, will list all keys
  <value>  Value to set
           if not specified, will get the value of the key

Options:
  -f, --force     Set value even if it is not existing
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Options:

result.stdout:
Description:
  Generate self-signed root CA

Usage:
  esign self-sign [options]

Options:
  -f, --force                                     Generate new self-signed root CA even if one already exists
  -cn, --commonName <commonName>                  Common Name for the certificate (e.g., example.com)
                                                  If not specified, the user will be prompted for input.
  -e, --email <email>                             Email address (e.g., support@example.com)
  -o, --organization <organization>               Organization name (e.g., Example Inc.)
  -ou, --organizationalUnit <organizationalUnit>  Organizational Unit (e.g., IT Department)
  -l, --locality <locality>                       Locality (e.g., New York)
  -st, --state <state>                            State or Province (e.g., NY)
  -c, --country <country>                         Country (e.g., US)
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:

Options:

  -cn, --commonName <commonName>                  Common Name for the certificate (e.g., example.com)
                                                  If not specified, the user will be prompted for input.
  -e, --email <email>                             Email address (e.g., support@example.com)
  -o, --organization <organization>               Organization name (e.g., Example Inc.)
  -ou, --organizationalUnit <organizationalUnit>  Organizational Unit (e.g., IT Department)
  -l, --locality <locality>                       Locality (e.g., New York)
  -st, --state <state>                            State or Province (e.g., NY)
  -c, --country <country>                         Country (e.g., US)
  -?, -h, --help                                  Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Sign bundle with certificate

Usage:
  esign sign [<bundle>] [options]

Arguments:
  <bundle>  Bundle path or directory containing the bundle
            if the bundle name is not specified, a default name will be used

Options:
  --pfx <pfx>                    PFX File contains certificate and private key
  --pfx-password <pfx-password>  PFX File password
  --no-password                  Ignore PFX File password prompt
  -s, --self-sign                Sign with self-signed certificate
  --skip-verification            Skip verification of the certificate
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:
  Sign bundle with certificate

Usage:
  esign sign [<bundle>] [options]

Arguments:
  <bundle>  Bundle path or directory containing the bundle
            if the bundle name is not specified, a default name will be used

Options:
  --pfx <pfx>                    PFX File contains certificate and private key
  --pfx-password <pfx-password>  PFX File password
  --no-password                  Ignore PFX File password prompt
  -s, --self-sign                Sign with self-signed certificate
  --skip-verification            Skip verification of the certificate
  -?, -h, --help                 Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Manage trusted root CAs and intermediate CAs

Usage:
  esign trust [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <path>   Add trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate
  list         List trusted root CA and intermediate CA certificates
  remove <ID>  Remove trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate

commands.payload:
Description:
  Manage trusted root CAs and intermediate CAs

Usage:
  esign trust [command] [options]

Options:
  -?, -h, --help  Show help and usage information

Commands:
  add <path>   Add trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate
  list         List trusted root CA and intermediate CA certificates
  remove <ID>  Remove trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate

result.stdout:
Description:
  Add trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate

Usage:
  esign trust add <path> [options]

Arguments:
  <path>  Path to the certificate file in PEM or DER format

Options:
  -i, --intermediate  Run command for Intermediate CA
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:

Options:
  -i, --intermediate  Run command for Intermediate CA
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  List trusted root CA and intermediate CA certificates

Usage:
  esign trust list [options]

Options:
  -i, --intermediate  Run command for Intermediate CA
  -v, --verbose       Show detailed information about the certificate
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

commands.payload:
Description:

Usage:

Options:

  -v, --verbose       Show detailed information about the certificate
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Remove trusted root CA or intermediate CA certificate

Usage:
  esign trust remove <ID> [options]

Arguments:
  <ID>  ID of the certificate

Options:
  -i, --intermediate  Run command for Intermediate CA
  -?, -h, --help      Show help and usage information

result.stdout:
Description:
  Verify bundle

Usage:
  esign verify [<bundle>] [options]

Arguments:
  <bundle>  Bundle path or directory containing the bundle
            if the bundle name is not specified, a default name will be used

Options:
  -i, --ignore-time  Ignore time validation
  -?, -h, --help     Show help and usage information
